Set against the picturesque landscapes of Turkey, Arafta unravels a tale of love forged in the flames of vengeance. Ateş, a man haunted by childhood scars and consumed by rage, returns to destroy the family that shattered his life years ago.

His weapon is not a sword, but a marriage: 187 days with Mercan, the daughter of his enemy. Mercan, already promised to Nezir, is thrust into a battle she never chose, while Ateş binds her to him believing she is the key to his revenge.

Yet the woman he intends to destroy becomes the one who threatens to undo him. And in the shadows, Aslı – driven by jealousy, seizes every chance to tear them apart. Suspended between love’s salvation and fate’s cruelty, Arafta is a saga of passion and payback, where every choice risks turning fire into ashes.

Jasmine tells the story of a young woman battling a life-threatening heart condition.

Yasemin’s only support in life is her stepbrother Tufan, who loves her with an obsessive devotion. As she struggles to get on the transplant list, Yasemin is pulled into a dark and dangerous world—one that drags both siblings toward an irreversible end.

Dilek, a mother of two who lives apart from her husband but is not yet divorced, works as an unlicensed taxi driver to make a living. One day, she picks up a passenger who’s taking an illegal taxi for the first time — and they get caught in a police check.

After losing her car, Dilek finds herself cornered by pressure from her husband and family during the divorce process. But she refuses to give up.

Just when she’s about to lose hope, the passenger from that day, Şadi, offers her a job as his driver. Accepting the offer pulls Dilek into a dark and dangerous world, where she will uncover hidden talents and ultimately change her own destiny.

The Şerzanoğlus are one of the oldest and most powerful tribes in Mardin. Over the years, their accumulated wealth and influence have made them one of the most notable families not only in the region but in all of Turkey. But behind this empire lie dark secrets, deep wounds, and shattered lives.

The family’s eldest son, Yaman Şerzanoğulları, is a just, principled, and compassionate man—unlike his father, Şeyhmuz. Yaman marries a woman his father does not approve of and turns his back on everything. However, his father’s pressure and schemes tear away the marriage he built on love and force him into another marriage approved by Şeyhmuz.

Years later, Yaman learns that he has a daughter from his first wife. As he sets out in search of his former love and child, a terrible accident changes everything. Yaman loses his memory, his former wife Zerhun disappears, and their daughter Hayat is left in the middle of the devastation. Zerhun’s mysterious disappearance, Hayat’s arrival at the Şerzanoğulları mansion, and the long-buried secrets that begin to surface one by one set off an irreversible chain of events within the family. Each secret brings a new conflict, and each confrontation uncovers a forgotten memory.

Originally known for their textile business, the Basmacıgil Family rises to global power with their groundbreaking invention: bulletproof thermal underwear. This achievement grants them the title of the Eighth Family, one of the ruling forces of the world.

Six half-siblings now face the daunting task of protecting their family’s new status. Yet, their clumsy attempts at crisis management, decision-making, and problem-solving—despite being driven by good intentions—push their father, Erol Basmacıgil, to lament: “We don’t need outside enemies.” When the real threat finally emerges, the siblings rediscover their bond. Under Erol’s leadership, the Basmacıgil Family engages in an intense struggle to defend their power against enemies both outside and within.

Aydan (Seda Bakan) finds herself as the owner of a luxury beauty center while working as a hairdresser in a working-class neighborhood of Istanbul. Her new boss is Emir (Uğur Güneş), a mysterious businessman whom no one knows anything about.

While trying to adapt to Emir's world, Aydan also has to deal with the troubles caused by her reluctant partner, the orphanage-raised, free-spirited Çiğdem (Ahsen Eroğlu). The path of the fearless commissioner Efe (Emre Bey), who is following Emir, inevitably crosses with Çiğdem and the hairdresser. While Aydan tries to find solace in her best friend, the manicurist Fiko (Şebnem Bozoklu), amidst all this chaos, her ex-husband, the talented hairdresser Tarık (Celil Nalçakan), who cannot stomach Aydan's new luxurious world, confronts them with a new problem every day.

Dogan is a handsome and successful surgeon living in Izmir. When he was young, a shocking incident with his father, Tahir Hancıoglu, caused him to break away from his family and leave Istanbul behind. In doing so, he left not only his family, but also his childhood. Now fully devoted to saving lives as a surgeon, Dogan has almost no connection with his family anymore. The only person he still stays close to is his younger sister, Melek.

After years away, Dogan returns to Istanbul for the first time at Melek’s insistence, to attend his sister Gulusan’s wedding. His return, however, brings back long-buried traumas and hidden crimes within the Hancıoğlu family. During this time, Dogan meets Cagla, a young lawyer who applies to work for the family company. Having grown up under the shadow of her sister Mahinur’s trauma, Cagla is strong, determined, and has a strong sense of justice. Their painful pasts create a deep connection between Dogan and Cagla.

The Hancıoglu family’s long-time enemy, Behram, is a man trying to survive, driven by his love for Mahinur. Now powerful, Behram watches the family from the shadows, waiting for the right moment to confront the past. An attempted murder on the wedding night changes everything. Old secrets begin to surface, and it becomes unclear who can be trusted. As events unfold, Dogan crosses paths with Cagla once again, and members of his own family start to appear among the suspects. Dogan is forced to make a choice: leave once more and return to his old life, or stay and face the truth by uncovering the secrets at the heart of his family.

Kemal, the debt collector for a loan shark family, sets out to collect the debt of Afife, a screenwriter who is forced to work in a restaurant. Along the way with her, Kemal discovers that virtue and love are not what he once believed.

In the streets of Istanbul, bodies discovered in brutally staged crime scenes put law enforcement on high alert.

After returning to duty following a difficult period, homicide detective Sare has only one clue: photographs of the victims left at fashion photographer Atlas’s home before each murder. As Sare and Atlas try to unravel the mystery behind the killings, they are drawn into a puzzle that makes them even doubt their own minds, chasing the truth through the dark waters of memory.

The series follows Haydar Ali (Deniz Can Aktaş), who, after avenging his family’s murder, ends up in prison.

There, he finds himself in the middle of one of Türkiye’s most dangerous underground cartels. However, the truly deadly confrontation begins three years later, when he regains his freedom and learns that the woman whom he has never been able to forget is now the wife of the man closest to him.

Deniz is an ambitious and cold-blooded expert rising in the finance sector. His relationship with his boss’s daughter Nilüfer, along with a partnership offer that crowns his career, seems to set him on a dazzling path. Meanwhile, he’s secretly trying to build a life with the true owner of his heart: Vicdan.

But the future he planned falls apart with a car accident. Vicdan disappears... and no one remembers she ever existed.

No security cameras, no neighbors, not even close friends… It’s as if Vicdan never lived. Only Deniz hasn’t forgotten her touch, her smile, her words. But what if she really never existed? Or if she did—but was erased? Vicdansız portrays love in its most fragile and vulnerable form.
Amid the onset of andropause, a 50-something family man becomes obsessed with change and decides to pursue happiness. Then he bungles it up completely.

The Town centers around two brothers who fell apart over the years, reunite in their small hometown after the death of their mother.

After discovering bags full of cash in the trunk of a crashed vehicle together with their loyal childhood friend, they find themselves stuck in a game of cat and mouse, squeezed between a rock and a hard place. Will they give up on their dreams or take the only shot at changing their lives?

Based on Orhan Pamuk’s internationally acclaimed novel, The Museum of Innocence tells the story of the enduring, tempestuous romance between Kemal, who comes from one of Istanbul’s wealthiest families, and his poor and distant young relation Füsun - an affair which begins in the 1970s after the two meet by chance.

Kemal, who is willing to take on the whole world for the sake of his love, starts collecting his beloved’s earrings, her hairclips, and even her discarded cigarette butts… What is love? An obsession, an affliction, an accident that derails the course of our life; or a great and innocent happiness?

Aynı Yağmur Altında follows Rosa, a young woman who has devoted her life to the struggle for human rights, whose world is turned upside down during a Gaza protest she attends in London. As she grapples with a serious illness and an unexpected personal quest, Rosa finds herself in Istanbul.

There, her path crosses once again with Ali, a man she met for the first time at the same protest. The two quickly fall in love, but their relationship will become the catalyst for a devastating chain of events that neither of them could foresee.

Sevdiğim Sensin tells the story of Dicle, a village girl whom Istanbul native Erkan saves from his cruel brothers by marrying her during his military service, and their journey that extends to Istanbul. Believing in this marriage with her pure heart, Dicle steps into Istanbul’s glittering yet harsh world, unaware of the planned marriage Erkan’s family wants for him.

The series, based on real events, follows a doctor who is forced to confront the fact that he did not become the person he once dreamed of being when he looks back on his life. After surviving a severe attack, Prof. Dr.

İnan Kural, the distinguished chief physician of Umutpark Medical Foundation Hospital, returns to life with no memory of the past 12 years. He finds himself in a world that feels completely unfamiliar. The years he lived are far from even being memories for him, and he struggles to recognize even himself.

Surrounded by unfamiliar relationships, incomprehensible bonds, and unanswered questions, he is forced to rebuild his life from scratch. For İnan, the past is now hidden behind a veil of fog; yet ahead of him lies a new path filled with choices, confrontations, and what he calls a “second chance.” Even if he cannot remember who he was, the courage to decide who he will become is still in his hands. This journey pushes him to reconnect with his profession as a doctor and to pursue the truth in another life, far from the traces of his old one.

Lal and Aktan are two people as different as night and day, yet one day they fall in love. Even though everything and everyone around them stands against their relationship, they can’t seem to let each other go.

Their love is both perfect and devastating—like a fairytale at times, like a nightmare at others. No matter how hard they try to break away after all they’ve been through, their love never ends. Asking the question, “Why do some loves never die?”, Bize Bi’şey Olmaz will confront viewers with the many faces of love.

Vefa Sultan focuses on Muslihuddin Mustafa’s journey toward becoming a spiritual guide after fulfilling the Hajj pilgrimage, and on his period as a mentor in Istanbul. Upon returning to the city, Vefa Sultan faces responsibilities that extend beyond the walls of the dervish lodge.

By guiding souls lost in the chaos of the growing city, he leads them back to their own truth. Portraying the great battle a person fights with their own ego in a striking way, the series presents a powerful spiritual journey along the delicate line between mercy and justice.

Having lost her family at a young age and faced the harsh realities of fate, Meryem (Derya Pınar Ak) has clung to memories of her childhood love, Kadir (Çağlar Ertuğrul).

Years later, Meryem, who clings to life with pure emotions, crosses paths again with Kadir, whose world is now shaped by power, money, and ambition. This encounter will bring about more than just a love story as secrets, confrontations, and irreversible decisions will be unravelled one after the other.

Delikanlı is a compelling journey through Istanbul, where resilience collides with the ruthless mechanics of power. The story follows Yusuf, a man defined by his unwavering integrity and deep-rooted loyalty.

Raised in a modest neighborhood, Yusuf dreams of a simple, honest life with his childhood love, Hazan. However, an unexpected clash with the world of privilege forces him into a life-altering crossroads.

Years later, Yusuf returns—not as a victim of his past, but as a man who has learned to play by the very rules that once destroyed him. Armed with a new identity and a calculated resolve, he begins to dismantle the lives of those who wronged him. Yet as he edges closer to his ultimate goal, he is confronted with a haunting question: in the pursuit of justice, how much of himself is he willing to lose?
